_note: MVP operator dashboard targets. Each target is generated into assets/operator-dashboards/<venue_slug>/<month>.json and surfaced via /operator/<venue_slug>. Report-aligned fields preserve the commercial facts from the detailed operator report so the client screen does not drift away from the report narrative.

report_aligned.public_visibility: Inside the public top 30 overall and category top 30
report_aligned.tracking_status: Strong public visibility; protect position and monitor nearby score clusters.

commercial_impact.wording_guardrail: Directional estimate only. Exact numbers require internal cover and spend data.

components.name: Trust & Compliance
components.summary: Trust & Compliance is strong. There is no immediate compliance-led commercial concern, but inspection recency and management-confidence signals should remain protected.

components.name: Customer Validation
components.summary: Strong Google evidence, but still single-platform. A second customer-platform source would improve evidence breadth and confidence.

components.name: Commercial Readiness
components.summary: Website, menu and opening hours are visible, but a direct booking/contact path is not confirmed in the public record.
components.evidence: Website present · menu present · hours 7/7 · booking/contact absent

priorities.title: Add a second customer-platform listing
priorities.why: Customer Validation is currently single-platform. A second source would improve confidence and reduce reliance on Google alone.
priorities.evidence: Only Google is present as a customer platform.
priorities.action: Claim or strengthen an additional legitimate review/listing source such as TripAdvisor or OpenTable where appropriate.
priorities.expected_upside: Raises the achievable confidence class toward Rankable-A.

priorities.title: Publish a reachable phone number or direct booking link
priorities.why: The booking/contact path carries 25% of Commercial Readiness and is not confirmed in the public record.
priorities.evidence: Public record does not show a confirmed direct booking/contact path.
priorities.action: Add a public phone number, direct reservation URL, or booking path to the main public listing.
priorities.expected_upside: Improves Commercial Readiness and reduces customer drop-off.
priorities.next_milestone: Add direct contact path to public listing

priorities.title: Defend review strength and score stability
priorities.why: Lambs is already in the public top 30 and restaurant category top 30, but it sits close to other high-scoring venues.
priorities.evidence: Nearby competitors sit at or near the same rounded RCS value.
priorities.action: Maintain legitimate review momentum and ensure listing accuracy does not degrade.
priorities.expected_upside: Protects rank within a tight score cluster.

what_not_to_do: Do not chase artificial reviews. Review growth should be legitimate and sustainable.
Do not assume photos, price level, social content, parking or delivery options directly move the score unless they connect to observable Commercial Readiness fields.
Do not over-focus on food-quality claims from compliance data; FHRS is a compliance signal, not a food-quality score.
report_aligned.final_takeaway: Lambs is already a high-performing and publicly visible operator in the DayDine Stratford-upon-Avon market. The commercial priority is to protect visibility and strengthen evidence breadth: add a second customer-platform source, improve the observed booking/contact path, and monitor the high-score cluster each month.
